programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Keith Jesperson, known as the Smiley Face Killer, killed eight women across several states in the 1990s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile into the murderer who terrorised the gay community as the 'Trash Bag Killer' and confessed to killing 32 victims.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A father took his children out for food after murdering his wife, then strangled them when they returned home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: William Bonin is thought to have raped, tortured and murdered a minimum of twenty-one boys and young men.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Joshua Wade had all the hall marks of a killer. We'll never understand the motives behind his random killings.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Mary Gough was found dead at the bottom of her stairs in her family home, her husband Colin Whelan appeared shocked. But a postmortem revealed her injuries weren't consistent with a fall. Whelan had murdered her for an insurance payout.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Remorseless murderer Casey Pigge's main ambition is to be the ultimate serial killer. If unrestrained, he remains a deadly threat.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Gary Vinter, in prison for double murder, stabs a fellow inmate in the eye and gives another brain damage.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Imprisoned after a botched robbery that ends with the fatal shooting of an innocent man, Craig Wissink later kills a corrections officer with a fire extinguisher in a brutal attack.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Denver Simmons is as dangerous as he is ruthless. Incarcerated for murdering a woman and her 14-year-old son, his violence only spiraled further once he was behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, construction manager Darrell North was found dead in his work trailer in Ft. Worth, Texas. When investigators arrived, they found that he had been stabbed 46 times.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two women went missing and were later found brutally murdered, police wondered if they were victims of a hate crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After inspecting storm damage to a home in Tampa, an insurance assessor simply disappeared. Thirty hours later, her body was found in a nearby river. But the killer had been careless.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The prime suspect had a criminal record and his driver's license was found at the scene of a brutal double homicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Christina Sanoubane was murdered in her Cedar Rapids apartment after moving away from her abusive boyfriend.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Fifteen years later, DNA technology leads to justice for a murdered woman and her savagely beaten 4-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the body of exotic dancer Rachel Siani was found beneath a New Jersey bridge. Investigators wondered if she had committed suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, teenager Tara Munsey went missing after a work shift at a Virginia restaurant. Did she run away, or was she the victim of foul play?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: There was no clear reason for a young, healthy college student to be dead. But when the coroner discovered a tiny clue during the autopsy, investigators were able to uncover a mystery.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police arrested a suspect for the death of a girl. But new technology proved the wrong man was behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2003, Tiffany Rowell and three of her friends were brutally murdered in affluent Clear Lake, Texas.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, Judy Southern arrived home from work and was shot by a gunman waiting within. Her husband Allen arrived shortly afterwards, called 911 to report his wife had been shot.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial rapist is on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman attends evening church services... then disappears. When her abandoned car is found, the tank is empty and a gas can she kept in her trunk for emergencies is missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2003, St. Cloud teenager Jason MacLennan returned home after a night out with friends and discovered his father Ken's body lying in a pool of blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a respected police officer was murdered in her own home. The crime went unsolved for more than a decade, until a newly formed cold case unit took a fresh look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1969, phone operator Diane Maxwell is murdered. Her brother promised he'd find out who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Tracey Frame, 35, was convicted of killing David Nixon in April 2002 after he planned to end their relationship.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An aspiring model turned up dead and the prime suspect was her boyfriend. When he was eventually cleared, investigators had to dig deeper to find the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim was a self-made man who never minced words. Perhaps he was murdered by a disgruntled client.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The medical examiner ruled a death an accident, but the detectives thought the evidence indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a college co-ed vanished without a trace, her fellow students were concerned about her safety and their own.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim has been stabbed more than thirty times, and the crime scene is awash with her blood. Near her head, police discover a distinctive button with strands of thread still attached.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Security cameras in a casino tracked a young woman's movements until shortly before she disappeared. She was never seen again.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The brutal murder of a state park employee over $2,000 rattles her co-workers and tourists while leaving police to sift through hundreds of leads.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lives changed in the 20 years following an unsolved murder, and so did forensic science. In time, a high-powered microscope and DNA profiling revealed a clue no one had seen before.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A bullet-riddled car, a missing driver, and no witnesses, an ambush or a random attack; the clue was something so tiny, it was measured in millionths of a meter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Michigan State University grad student disappeared and was presumed dead. With the help of a professor of geological sciences, police hoped to get the "dirt" on her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman in the back of the truck was flailing her arms, screaming. They thought she was doing something dangerous for the fun of it.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had ingested a massive amount of cyanide. An unlikely clue - a flaw on a mailing envelope - exposed a murderer who was willing to kill innocent people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Kathy Lorick was murdered on a Concord, California jogging trail while talking to her husband on her phone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Derrick Duehren returned to find his Oregon home burned to the ground. His wife's charred remains were later found in the rubble.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2001, Ginger Hayes and her infant son Nicholas were abducted during a carjacking and the crime had been reported by a witness within minutes of occurrence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, funeral director Lonnie Turner, Sr. was found shot to death. His son Lonnie Jr. became the prime suspect, particularly after it was discovered that the murder was committed with his gun. However, he had an alibi for the time of the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman is found dead in her apartment. With little evidence at the scene, the case turns cold.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Detectives search for the bombers of two churches in Illinois, hoping that the materials used in the remnants of the handmade bombs will offer the clues to catch the culprits.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On May 12, 1994, Crystal Perry was viciously murdered in her home in Bridgton, Maine. Michael Hutchinson, 32, was found guilty of murder in the 1994 slaying at Perry's home.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Detectives and deputies arrest sex traffickers in a prostitution sting operation. A store owner reports a suspicious man looking into car windows. A deputy is called to a business where four people sitting in a car appear to be up to no good.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Follow a SWAT mission from briefing to execution. A man wanted for assault with a deadly weapon is taken into custody.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police attempt to arrest a drug dealer. A young woman is found lying on the side of the road. Officers try to catch people who are willing to buy alcohol for minors.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman is stopped for driving under the influence. A mentally unbalanced young man arms himself and refuses to cooperate with law enforcement.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A father, worried about the behavior of his 15-year-old son, takes him to visit the El Dorado County Jail.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A driver is passed out with his vehicle moving in reverse. Boat patrol officers find an inflatable boat that is losing air. Deputies respond to a call from a homeowner concerned about two suspicious individuals.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a homeowner's report that people were stealing items from a neighbor's house. A woman is found driving her car with no license plate and a flat tire. A deputy is flagged down by another motorist to report an accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police respond to a report of a stabbing at a housing complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Fire Is Coming, Time to Run!

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies investigate a report of a woman throwing objects at passing cars. According to a domestic violence call, an intoxicated woman throws heavy objects at her husband.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A driver is passed out with his vehicle moving in reverse. Boat patrol officers find an inflatable boat that is losing air. Deputies respond to a call from a homeowner concerned about two suspicious individuals.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputy responds to a report of shoplifting. The search of a motel room yields incriminating evidence.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies pick up people with PRCS warrants of $150,000 or more.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy tries to make contact with a parolee who has a history of fighting. Officers respond to an assault at a gas station. A husband allegedly assaults his wife.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a parolee who is locked in a residence and known to have a weapon. A trained dog finds clues that a human would surely miss.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman with felony warrants is staying at a known drug house. The occupants of a vehicle in a routine traffic stop claim to be sovereign citizens and that the law doesn't apply to them. A burglary at a business complex.

programme.sub-title: Buyer Beware of a Real Steal on Craigslist
programme.desc: A Special Enforcement Detail officer executes a plan to catch some burglars who ransacked several homes. A 9-1-1 caller reports that a woman broke into her car.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A man with multiple arrests for heroin possession talks about what life on the drug is really like.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of assault with a bat and pepper spray. Illicit drug use is reported to be going on in a feed store parking lot.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police seek to take down a known drug house where they expect many arrests. Deputies investigate a van parked after hours in a school parking area. Knives, bullets and marijuana are found in a suspicious car.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a 9-1-1 call about a possible burglary. Deputies answer a domestic violence call, where a man claims his wife choked him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responding to a call in the outlying area of the county finds a large illegal marijuana grow operation. An officer investigates several reports of petty theft.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A boater is reported to be under the influence of drugs or alcohol. Open containers and drugs are found in a suspicious vehicle. Deputies investigate individuals stealing recycling material.

programme.sub-title: Pot, Pipe Bombs and Clueless People
programme.desc: Police execute a search warrant on a marijuana grow operation run by illegal Mexican nationals. A person reports they found three pipe bombs. In a domestic violence call, a man is reported to be burning all his own property on his front lawn.

programme.sub-title: Nine-Time Loser Goes for His Tenth!

programme.desc: A couple is caught having sex in a place where no one is supposed to be. A bag of highly explosive material is found attached to a mailbox. A drone spots a large marijuana growing site. A man puts his fist through a motel window.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of a man and woman fighting. Deputies investigate a reported verbal disturbance outside an apartment complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies struggle with a wanted man who had too much to drink. A deputy deals with two young thieves. A young man bleeding from the head gives an officer a difficult time.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy checks in on a woman who is on probation and is about to be evicted from the motel room she's living in. A man's neighbors claim he is driving drunk. A traffic stop leads to a drug charge.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police execute a search for heroin in a horrifically filthy house. A report of suspicious activity in a grocery store parking lot. Officers investigate an abandoned trailer.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A 9-1-1 call about a man threatening to kill his family. The investigation of a shoplifting case leads police to over 66 grams of black tar heroin. A domestic violence call.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A vehicle has no front license plates and an expired registration. Two juveniles are reportedly intoxicated. Deputies are informed when a child predator is release from jail.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
